---
title: "02_ADNI_ADRC_harmonized"
author: "Raymond Otoo"
date: "2025-11-13"
output: pdf_document
---
#=====================================================================================
#
# 00- Set working directory
#=====================================================================================
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
source("project_setup.R")
paths <- init_project(knitr_root = TRUE)
getwd()
```


```{r}
library(sva)
library(limma)
library(openxlsx)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
```


```{r}
PhenoInfo_numeric <- read.xlsx(file.path(paths$results_dir, "PhenoInfo_numeric.xlsx"))
# Phenotypic data
ProtoData_filtered <- read.xlsx(file.path(paths$results_dir, "ProtoData_filtered.xlsx"))   

PhenoInfo_numeric
ProtoData_filtered
```

```{r}
# Proteomics Data
ProtoData_filtered <- ProtoData_filtered %>%
  rename(SampleID = 1) %>%
  mutate(SampleID = trimws(SampleID))
 
# Phenotype Data
PhenoInfo_numeric <- PhenoInfo_numeric %>%
  rename(SampleID = 1) %>%
  mutate(SampleID = trimws(SampleID))

PhenoInfo_numeric
ProtoData_filtered
```


```{r}
# Set Row Names for alignment and remove the SampleID column from the phenotype data.
# Note: The expression data will be transposed later, so we keep SampleID as a column for now.
rownames(PhenoInfo_numeric) <- PhenoInfo_numeric$SampleID
PhenoInfo_numeric <- PhenoInfo_numeric %>% select(-SampleID)

```


```{r}
PhenoInfo_numeric
ProtoData_filtered
```

```{r}
# Ensure Cohort is a factor for ComBat
PhenoInfo_numeric$Cohort <- as.factor(PhenoInfo_numeric$Cohort)
```

```{r}
PhenoInfo_numeric
```

```{r}
dim(PhenoInfo_numeric)
dim(ProtoData_filtered)
```
#Prepare Expression matrix
```{r}
# Step 2a: Convert to strictly numeric matrix (ComBat requirement)
# Use the Proto data *without* the SampleID column for matrix conversion.
expr_data_for_matrix <- ProtoData_filtered %>% select(-c(SampleID,drawDate,Panel.OLINK))
expr_matrix <- as.matrix(sapply(expr_data_for_matrix, as.numeric))
rownames(expr_matrix) <- ProtoData_filtered$SampleID # Re-assign clean IDs to the matrix
 
# Step 2b: Transpose (Proteins as Rows, Samples as Columns)
expr_matrix_transposed <- t(expr_matrix)
# Step 2c: Handle NAs in the Transposed Matrix
all_na_rows <- which(rowSums(is.na(expr_matrix_transposed)) == ncol(expr_matrix_transposed))
if(length(all_na_rows) > 0) {
    expr_matrix_clean <- expr_matrix_transposed[-all_na_rows, ]
    print(paste("Removed", length(all_na_rows), "proteins that were all NA."))
} else {
    expr_matrix_clean <- expr_matrix_transposed
}
```


#Define batch and design
```{r}
# --- CRITICAL FIX 2: ALIGNMENT USING MATCH() ---
# 1. Define the exact order of samples from the clean matrix
sample_order <- colnames(expr_matrix_clean)

# 2. Use match() to find the index of these samples in the phenotype data
match_index <- match(sample_order, rownames(PhenoInfo_numeric))

# 3. Create the phenotype data frame and batch vector in the correct, guaranteed order
pheno_combat <- PhenoInfo_numeric[match_index, ]
batch_vector <- pheno_combat$Cohort 

# Null Model: Adjust only for the batch variable (Cohort)
mod_null <- model.matrix(~1, data = pheno_combat)


# ===== PCA BEFORE BATCH CORRECTION =====
library(ggplot2)
# 1. Run PCA on the expression matrix BEFORE batch correction
# Assuming Proto_before_z is your standardized matrix prior to ComBat
pca_before <- prcomp(t(expr_matrix_clean), scale. = TRUE)
 
# 2. Prepare data frame for plotting
pca_df_before <- data.frame(
  PC1 = pca_before$x[, 1],
  PC2 = pca_before$x[, 2],
  Cohort = pheno_combat$Cohort
)
 
# 3. Create the plot object
pca_plot_before <- ggplot(pca_df_before, aes(PC1, PC2, color = Cohort)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  ggtitle("PCA Before ComBat: Visible Cohort Effects") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
 
# 4. Display the plot
print(pca_plot_before)
 
# 5. Save the plot
ggsave(filename = file.path(paths$plots_dir, "PCA_Before_Batch_Correction.png"), 
       plot = pca_plot_before, 
       width = 8, 
       height = 6, 
       dpi = 300)
 
# 6. Check variance explained
print("Variance Explained Before Batch Correction:")
summary(pca_before)$importance[2, 1:3]
```


#Run CoBat and Finalize
```{r}
# Run ComBat
combat_adjusted_matrix <- ComBat(
    dat = expr_matrix_clean, 
    batch = batch_vector, 
    mod = mod_null,
    par.prior = TRUE, 
    prior.plots = FALSE 
)
# Transpose back (Samples as Rows, Proteins as Columns)
Proto_Combat_Adjusted <- as.data.frame(t(combat_adjusted_matrix))

# ------------------This was Re-normalizing the matrix as this has already been done---------------------

# Re-Zscore the batch-adjusted data for comparability
#Proto_combat_z <- as.data.frame(scale(Proto_Combat_Adjusted))
# ------------------This was Re-normalizing the matrix as this has already been done---------------------

Proto_combat_z <- Proto_Combat_Adjusted 
 
# Save output
write.xlsx(Proto_combat_z, file.path(paths$results_dir, "ADNI_ADRC_Proteomics_Combat_Adjusted_Zscored.xlsx"), rowNames = TRUE)
```


## PCA AFTER BATCH CORRECTION 
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
 
# 1. Create a directory for your plots if it doesn't exist
if(!dir.exists("plots")) dir.create("plots")
 
# 2. Ensure sample order matches phenotype data
Proto_combat_z <- Proto_combat_z[rownames(pheno_combat), ]
 
# 3. Run PCA
pca_after <- prcomp(Proto_combat_z, scale. = TRUE)
 
# 4. Prepare data frame
pca_df_after <- data.frame(
  PC1 = pca_after$x[, 1],
  PC2 = pca_after$x[, 2],
  Cohort = pheno_combat$Cohort
)
 
# 5. Create the plot object
pca_plot <- ggplot(pca_df_after, aes(PC1, PC2, color = Cohort)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  ggtitle("PCA After ComBat: Cohort Effect Removed") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
 
# 6. Display the plot in RStudio
print(pca_plot)
 
# 7. SAVE the plot as a high-resolution PNG
# Adjust width/height as needed for your paper/report
ggsave(filename = file.path(paths$plots_dir, "PCA_After_Batch_Correction.png"), 
       plot = pca_plot, 
       width = 8, 
       height = 6, 
       dpi = 300)
 
# 8. Output Importance Summary to console
print("Variance Explained After Batch Correction:")
summary(pca_after)$importance[2, 1:3]
```

```{r}
# For multiple objects:
save(pheno_combat, Proto_combat_z , file = file.path(paths$results_dir, "02_output.RData"))
```
